reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissibility: what can and cannot be concluded when ARC-fitted kinetics diverge drastically from DSC-inferred ranges, while avoiding forcing mechanism claims. Decoys are largely single-fault and plausibly tempting (invalidating ARC fit, inferring mechanism change, asserting insensitivity), creating meaningful expert discrimination. Minor weakness: option A’s “fundamental limitation” phrasing can feel slightly stronger than necessary given it depends on interpreting the DSC range as truly inaccessible rather than merely not observed in typical inference.
